name: Briefing Note
description: Produce a draft briefing note on a policy topic for senior executive review. Follows standard APS briefing note conventions with built-in quality checks for factual accuracy, sceptical review, and compliance.

inputs_required.name: topic
inputs_required.label: Topic
inputs_required.description: The policy topic for the briefing note

inputs_required.name: audience
inputs_required.label: Audience
inputs_required.description: Who will read this (e.g. First Assistant Secretary, Deputy Secretary)

inputs_required.name: purpose
inputs_required.label: Purpose
inputs_required.description: What the brief is for (e.g. Senate Estimates prep, stakeholder meeting, decision)

inputs_required.name: policy_area
inputs_required.label: Policy area
inputs_required.description: The specific policy domain you work in

inputs_required.name: length
inputs_required.label: Target length
inputs_required.description: How long should the brief be?

inputs_required.name: key_documents
inputs_required.label: Reference documents (optional)
inputs_required.description: Paste or summarise any relevant documents, data, or context you want the model to work from
inputs_required.placeholder: Paste document text or a summary here...

steps.name: Scope & Research
steps.description: Identify the key issues, background context, stakeholders, and potential sensitivities relevant to the topic.
steps.user_action_after: Review the issues list critically. Remove items that don't apply to your context. Add anything missing based on your own knowledge — your domain expertise matters most at this stage.

steps.name: Structure
steps.description: Propose a briefing note structure using APS conventions, tailored to your audience and purpose.
steps.user_action_after: Confirm the structure works for your audience and purpose. Adjust headings or emphasis as needed. This is worth checking with your supervisor if you're unsure.

steps.name: Draft
steps.description: Generate the full briefing note draft using APS writing conventions, with explicit [VERIFY] flags for uncertain claims.
steps.user_action_after: Read the draft carefully. Note anything that feels wrong, missing, or that you couldn't defend if asked. Look for [VERIFY] and [INSERT DATA] flags — these are your action items.

platform_tips.copilot: For this drafting step, use Copilot in Word rather than Copilot Chat — it can work directly with the document structure and apply formatting. In Word, use 'Draft with Copilot' to generate the initial brief, then refine with follow-up prompts.

steps.name: Claim Verification
steps.description: Extract and categorise all factual claims in the draft. Makes the trust boundary visible before you rely on the content.
steps.user_action_after: Work through the verification table. Check flagged items against authoritative sources (legislation, agency websites, official statistics). Resolving [VERIFY] items is your accountability step under the PGPA Act — you are the officer responsible for the advice.

platform_tips.copilot: Start a fresh Copilot message for this step. Copy the full draft text and paste it directly — do not assume Copilot has retained it from the drafting step.

steps.name: Red-Team Challenge
steps.description: Stress-test the draft from a sceptical senior executive perspective. Surfaces logic gaps and missing perspectives.
steps.user_action_after: Review the critique. Use your judgment on which issues to address — not every point will apply to your context. Focus on the Top 3 priority issues identified.

platform_tips.copilot: Start a fresh Copilot message. Paste the full draft text alongside this prompt — Copilot's red-team analysis is most reliable when all context is included in the current message.

steps.name: Revise
steps.description: Revise the draft incorporating verification findings and challenge feedback. Produces an improved version ready for final compliance check.
steps.user_action_after: Review the revised brief. Ensure all [VERIFY] and [INSERT DATA] placeholders are resolved before the final step. If any remain, resolve them now.

platform_tips.copilot: If you used Copilot in Word for the drafting step, return to that document for the revision. Otherwise, include the full current draft and the key feedback from Steps 4 and 5 in this prompt.

steps.name: Compliance Check
steps.description: Check the revised brief against PGPA Act, APS Values, Privacy Act, PSPF, and FOI requirements.
steps.user_action_after: Address any compliance issues flagged. If you are uncertain about any compliance matter, check with your supervisor or your agency's legal/governance team. This is your final sign-off check before submission.

platform_tips.copilot: Start a fresh Copilot message for this compliance check. Paste the full revised brief — compliance checking is most reliable as a focused, standalone prompt.
platform_tips.chatgpt: If this step feels generic, re-state the specific frameworks to check against (PGPA Act, Privacy Act, APS Values, PSPF, FOI Act) in the prompt before copying.

beginner.notes: Start with research, drafting, and claim verification. The structure and red-team steps add value but increase complexity — try them once you're comfortable. The compliance check (Step 7) is always worth running.

intermediate.notes: Run the full workflow. The user actions after each step are where your expertise adds the most value — read them carefully.

advanced.notes: Consider adding parallel analysis chains (economic, legal, social perspectives) after Step 1. Customise the role definitions for your specific branch. Run the claim verification gate on both the initial and revised drafts.

worked_example.scenario: APS 6 policy officer in the Department of Social Services preparing a briefing note on NDIS participant satisfaction trends for a First Assistant Secretary ahead of a stakeholder roundtable.
worked_example.note: This is a fictitious scenario using publicly available information only.

claude.setup_guidance: Create a Claude Project called 'Policy Drafting'. Paste the instructions below into the Project Instructions field. You can also upload reference documents (agency style guides, previous briefs, legislation extracts) to the Project knowledge base — they will be available in every conversation automatically.
claude.content:
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) policy analyst at the EL1 level. You write in formal APS style — concise, evidence-based, and impartial. You understand standard APS document conventions including briefing notes, QT briefs, and cabinet submissions.

You are familiar with accountability obligations under the PGPA Act and information-handling requirements under the PSPF and Privacy Act.

When drafting content:
- Flag uncertain claims with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps requiring human input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not fabricate statistics, program details, legislative references, or named individuals' roles
- If you are uncertain about a fact, say so explicitly rather than guessing
- Keep language plain and direct — avoid jargon unless it is standard APS terminology
claude.document_handling: Upload reference documents (agency style guides, previous briefing notes, relevant legislation extracts) to the Project knowledge base. They will be available in every conversation within the Project without re-uploading.

chatgpt.setup_guidance: Create a ChatGPT Project called 'Policy Drafting'. Paste the instructions below into the Project instructions field. These will apply to all conversations within the Project, replacing your global Custom Instructions for this context.
chatgpt.content:
## Role
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) policy analyst at the EL1 level. You write in formal APS style — concise, evidence-based, and impartial.

## Standards
You understand standard APS document conventions including briefing notes, QT briefs, and cabinet submissions. You are familiar with accountability obligations under the PGPA Act and information-handling requirements under the PSPF and Privacy Act.

## Rules
- Flag uncertain claims with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps requiring human input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not fabricate statistics, program details, or legislative references
- If you are uncertain about a fact, say so explicitly
- Be concise — use plain APS English, not jargon
- Avoid excessive bullet points; use flowing paragraphs for policy advice
chatgpt.document_handling: Upload reference documents directly to your ChatGPT Project so they persist across conversations. Reference files by name in your prompts for best results (e.g. 'Using the style guide I uploaded...').

copilot.setup_guidance: Copilot does not support persistent custom instructions. Paste the context block below at the start of each workflow session before running Step 1. Keep your reference documents in SharePoint or OneDrive and use the / reference key to include them in your prompts.
copilot.content:
I need you to act as an experienced APS policy analyst at the EL1 level. Write in formal APS style — concise, evidence-based, and impartial. Follow standard briefing note conventions.

Rules:
- Flag uncertain facts with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps needing my input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not guess at statistics, legislative details, or program specifics — if you are not sure, say so
- Be concise and direct
copilot.document_handling: Keep reference documents in SharePoint or OneDrive. Use the / key in Copilot Chat to reference them directly in your prompts. For detailed drafting, open the document in Word and use Copilot from within Word rather than Copilot Chat.

other.setup_guidance: Paste the instructions below at the start of each new conversation before running the workflow steps. This establishes the context and constraints your AI tool should follow throughout the session.
other.content:
Role: You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) policy analyst at the EL1 level.

Instructions: Write in formal APS style — concise, evidence-based, and impartial. Follow standard briefing note conventions.

Rules:
- Flag uncertain facts with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps needing input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not guess at statistics, legislative details, or program specifics
- If you are uncertain about a fact, say so explicitly
other.document_handling: Paste relevant document content (or summaries) directly into the prompts at the steps where you need to reference them.

known_limitations: Cannot access current departmental data, internal reports, or classified information.
Statistics and program details may be outdated or fabricated — always verify before including in any official document.
The draft follows general APS conventions but may not match your agency's specific template or style guide.
This workflow produces AI-assisted drafts. You remain the accountable officer for the advice.
